calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Order
Off Calendar Jul-08-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Suzanne Dimeff appeared by telephone for Bill Sonneman (Benificiary) present and Jane Sonneman (Trustee) present. Seth Skootsky for Joan Quinn (Moving Party).

The Parties presented oral argument to support there positions. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith and A. Santiago.

Petition To Compel Accounting; For Removal Of Jane Sonneman As Trustee; For Appointment Of Debra J Dolch As Successor Trustee; And For Redress Of Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Apr-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. SETH M. SKOOTSKY APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER AND BENEFICIARY;
ATTY. SUZANNE DIMEFF FOR JANE SONNEMAN, TRUSTEE BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

MR. SKOOTSKY REPORTED TO THE COURT THAT HE HAD NOTHING TO ADD TO THE STATUS REPORT FILED BY HIM. MS. DIMEFF ON THE OTHER HAND REMINDED THE COURT THAT THIS CASE WAS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION FROM THE HEARING OF FEBRUARY 26, 2008 AS TO WHO HAS JURISDICTION OVER THIS MATTER WHETHER SAN DIEGO OR SAN FRANCISCO? THE COURT APOLOGIZED FOR NOT ISSUING AN ORDER. SAN DIEGO COUNTY HAS THE EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION AND WILL ISSUE AN ORDER TO THAT EFFECT. MR. SKOOTSKY IS REQUESTING FOR A STATEMENT OF DECISION AND THE COURT WILL LOOK INTO IT WHETHER HE IS ENTITLED TO ONE. MS. DIMEFF TO PREPARE ORDER.
JUDGE: JOHN DEARMAN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT/SAJJA RAVINANTAPRICHA REPORTER: KATH BERSAMIN, CSR # 11722

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Issue Of Exclusive Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Apr-28-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Suzanne Dimeff for and with Jane Sonneman. Seth Skootsky for Jaon Quinn (Petitioner).

The Court stated the tentative: San Francisco does not have jurisdiction. Mr. Skootsky argued that the petitions were filed 5 months apart, that Ms. Quinn's petition was filed first, and cited case law to support his position. Ms. Dimeff stated that the case cited was inapplicable, that the first petition was a heggsted petition, and that the day to day administration was in San Diego county. In addition, she stated that 50% of the owners and the agents were there. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.

Petition To Compel Accounting; For Removal Of Jane Sonneman As Trustee; For Appointment Of Debra J Dolch As Successor Trustee; And For Redress Of Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Jan-22-2008 Continued To Apr-08-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Skootsky (by phone) for Joan Quinn (Beneficiary). Suzanne Dimeff for and with Jane Sonneman (Trustee). William Sonneman (Beneficiary) in Propria Perona.

Mr. Skootsky reported that there was no settlement and requested the court order an accounting. Ms. Dimeff requested the court vacate the May 1st order stating that she did not receive notice and that the issues were now mute. In addition, she reported that there was a motion to change venue on March 13, 2008 and a petition for plan of distribution on March 14, 2008 pending before Judge Kline. Mr. Skootsky objected stating that there was no motion before the court to vacate the order and that this court still had jurisdiction. All matters were continued to April 08, 2008. The issue of exclusive jurisdiction was set for February 26, 2008 at 10:30 a.m. The Court ordered all breifs to filed before February 20, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

Petition To Compel Accounting; For Removal Of Jane Sonneman As Trustee; For Appointment Of Debra J Dolch As Successor Trustee; And For Redress Of Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Jan-08-2008 Continued To Jan-22-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Accounting; For Removal Of Jane Sonneman As Trustee; For Appointment Of Debra J Dolch As Successor Trustee; And For Redress Of Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Oct-31-2007 Continued To Jan-08-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Suzanne Dimeff for and with Jane Sonneman (Trustee). Seth Skootsky for Joan Quinn (Petitioner).

Ms. Dimeff requested a continuance, reported that her client was medically unable to attend, and that an appraisal had just been received. Mr. Skootsky objected to the continuance. The Court continued the matter to January 08, 2007.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Doug Handa.

Petition To Compel Accounting; For Removal Of Jane Sonneman As Trustee; For Appointment Of Debra J Dolch As Successor Trustee; And For Redress Of Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Aug-21-2007 Continued To Oct-31-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: SETH SKOOTSKY, APPEARING FOR AND JOAN QUINN, SUZANNE DIMEFF, APPEARING FOR AND WITH JANE SONNEMAN, ERNEST DER, APPEARING FOR JOAN QUINN

MR. SKOOTSKY STATES THAT THERE HAS BEEN NO MEDIATION ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT REQUEST MEDIATION, THAT IS TO BE COMPLETED ON OR BEFORE 10/17/07 AND IS SET FOR STATUS ON 10/31/07.

JUDGE: JOHN DEARMAN; REPORTER: KATY BERSAMIN,CSR#11722,CLERK: LARRISA DOTSON; BALIFF: M. LOOS

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Order
Off Calendar Aug-21-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: SETH SKOOTSKY, APPEARING FOR AND JOAN QUINN, SUZANNE DIMEFF, APPEARING FOR AND WITH JANE SONNEMAN, ERNEST DER, APPEARING FOR JOAN QUINN
MS. DIMEFF REQUEST THAT THE MATTER NOT BE DISCUSSED IN OPEN COURT PER THE WISHES OF THE TRUSTOR. AFTER ASKING PARTIES APPEARING TODAY AND DISCOVERING THAT THEY ARE FAMILY MEMBERS THE REQUEST WAS DENIED BY THE JUDGE. COUNSEL RAISES THE ISSUE OF VENUE, STATING THAT MOST OF THE PROPERTY, THE CPA AND APPRAISERS ALL RESIDE IN SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA. MR. SKOOTSKY STATES THAT THERE IS NO CASE LAW TO SUPPORT THE MOTION TO VACATE. HE ALSO STATES THAT THERE WAS NO WRITTEN REQUEST FOR ARBRITRATION. ALSO ON 5/1/07 THERE WAS AN ORDER TO VACATE THE SAN FRANCISCO PROPERTY AND LIST IT TO SELL. IT WAS LISTED, THEN SHORTLY AFTER TAKEN OFF THE MARKET. THEREFORE COUNSEL DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE COURT ORDER. ORDER DENIED BUT A STAY IN IS PLACE UNTIL PARTIES GO TO MEDIATION REGARDING OTHER MATTER.
JUDGE: JOHN DEARMAN; REPORTER: KATY BERSAMIN,CSR#11722,CLERK: LARRISA DOTSON; BALIFF: M. LOOS

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Creshon.
